LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT LVI EVNIRONMENTAL SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO ESTABLISH THAT THE COMPLETED AND ACCEPTED DOCTRINE APPLIES BECAUSE THE EVIDENCE INDICATES THAT DEFENDANT WAS STILL WORKING ON THE THIRD FLOOR, THE SCENE OF THE ACCIDENT, AFTER THE INJURY. (CHANCE V. LAWYER'S INC. (1962) 58 CAL.2D 368). THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER THE HAZARD WAS OPEN AND OBVIOUS AND WHETHER PLAINTIFF ACTED UNREASONABLY WHEN PLACING HIS LADDER NEAR THE DEPRESSION. THE HIRER OF AN INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR IS NOT LIABLE TO AN EMPLOYEE OF THE CONTRACTOR MERELY BECAUSE THE HIRER RETAINED CONTROL OVER SAFETY CONDITIONS AT A WORKSITE, BUT A HIRER IS LIABLE TO AN EMPLOYEE OF A CONTRACTOR INSOFAR AS A HIRER'S EXERCISE OF RETAINED CONTROL AFFIRMATIVELY CONTRIBUTED TO THE EMPLOYEE'S INJURIES. HOOKER VS. DEPT. OF TRANSPORTATION (2002) 27 CAL.4TH 198, 202. DEFENDANT SHIFTS THE BURDEN BY SHOWING DEFENDANT DID NOT AFFIRMATIVELY CONTRIBUTE TO THE JURY. NO EVIDENCE PRESENTED THAT DEFENDANT TOOK ACTION THAT CONTRIBUTED TO THE INJURY. REQUEST FOR CONTINUANCE DENIED, DECLARATION NOT SUFFICIENT. CCP 437C(H). DEFENDANT LVI EVNIRONMENTAL SERVICES, INC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
Denied. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT S.J. AMOROSO, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: Allan M. Tabor, Esq. of Ryan & Tabor (510) 444-5350 Appearing thru CourtCall for Plaintiff and opposing party; Julian Pardo de Zela appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is granted. The hirer of an independent contractor is not liable to an employee of the contractor merely because the hirer retained control over safety conditions at a worksite, but a hirer is liable to an employee of a contractor insofar as a hirer's exercise of retained control affirmatively contributed to the employee's injuries. Hooker vs. Dept. of Transportation (2002) 27 Cal.4th 198, 202. Defendant shifts the burden by showing defendant did not affirmatively contribute to the jury. No evidence presented that defendant took action that contributed to the injury. Request for continuance denied, Declaration not sufficient. CCP 437c(h). (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
